title: lithocarpus porcatus
intro: Lithocarpus porcatus, also known as the Piggyback Acorn or Piggyback Walnut, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Asia, for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Lithocarpus porcatus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Lithocarpus porcatus include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
